title: Comparator Item Detector
description: A comparator reads the fill level of a chest, hopper, or furnace and outputs a signal strength proportional to how full it is (0 = empty, 15 = full). Place the comparator directly beside the container with its back facing the container and its front facing away. Used in item sorters, storage monitors, and auto-crafters.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Four stone blocks in a line. The chest and comparator both sit at y=1; stone provides the surface. We need four positions: chest, comparator, output stone, and lamp.

steps.title: Chest and comparator
steps.explanation: Place a chest at (0,1,0). Place the comparator at (1,1,0) facing east — its back reads the chest to the west, its front outputs east. The comparator outputs a signal from 0 to 15 based on how many items are in the chest. Put some items in the chest now to verify signal is non-zero.

steps.title: Output dust and lamp
steps.explanation: Dust at (2,1,0) carries the comparator's output signal east. A redstone lamp at (3,1,0) lights up proportionally — fully lit at signal 15 (full chest), dark at signal 0 (empty). Use the dust connection point at (2,1,0) to wire this sensor into any larger circuit.
